channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: China slams Trump's trade investigation, as it approves a 5-year economic plan
item.description: China's Foreign Ministry criticized the Trump administration's trade investigation as a "pretext" for tariffs. Meanwhile, China is moving ahead with a five-year plan that may rankle trade partners.
item.content:encoded:
China's Foreign Ministry criticized the Trump administration's trade investigation as a "pretext" for tariffs. Meanwhile, China is moving ahead with a five-year plan that may rankle trade partners.

(Image credit: Ng Han Guan)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Pak

item.title: Senate passes bipartisan housing bill targeting large investors and easing regulations
item.description: The 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act would ban large investors from buying up single-family homes.
item.content:encoded:
The 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act would ban large investors from buying up single-family homes.

(Image credit: Kent Nishimura)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: This reporter went bust while covering America's sports betting boom
item.description: Americans are betting on sports, elections, award shows and even military actions. The Atlantic writer McKay Coppins bet $10k from his employer in his investigation of this gambling world.
item.content:encoded: Americans are betting on sports, elections, award shows and even military actions. The Atlantic writer McKay Coppins bet $10k from his employer in his investigation of this gambling world.
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: How the Iran war is disrupting air travel -- and advice if you're planning a trip
item.description: The war in Iran is roiling jet fuel prices and airlines are beginning to hike prices, unsettling travelers far from the Middle East. If you're booking a flight soon, here are things to know.
item.content:encoded:
The war in Iran is roiling jet fuel prices and airlines are beginning to hike prices, unsettling travelers far from the Middle East. If you're booking a flight soon, here are things to know.

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: Countries agree to a historic release of stockpiled oil to ease global disruption
item.description: Members of the International Energy Agency have announced a coordinated release of 400 million barrels of stockpiled oil in an attempt to counter the disruption in oil trade triggered by the Iran war.
item.content:encoded:
Members of the International Energy Agency have announced a coordinated release of 400 million barrels of stockpiled oil in an attempt to counter the disruption in oil trade triggered by the Iran war.

(Image credit: Omar Havana)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: 2025 saw relatively fewer natural disasters. Will you get a break on home insurance?
item.description: Disaster costs fell in the U.S. in 2025. Still, it was the fourth time in five years that extreme weather inflicted more than $100 billion in annual losses. Industry experts say the growing financial toll will make insurers wary of rushing to cut rates.
item.content:encoded:
Disaster costs fell in the U.S. in 2025. Still, it was the fourth time in five years that extreme weather inflicted more than $100 billion in annual losses. Industry experts say the growing financial toll will make insurers wary of rushing to cut rates.

(Image credit: ALLISON JOYCE/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Michael Copley

item.title: 'Pro-worker AI,' streaming fatalities, and other fascinating new economic studies
item.description: From artificial intelligence to fatalities from music streaming to the effects of immigrants on elderly health care, the Planet Money newsletter rounds up some interesting new economic studies.
item.content:encoded:
From artificial intelligence to fatalities from music streaming to the effects of immigrants on elderly health care, the Planet Money newsletter rounds up some interesting new economic studies.

(Image credit: KIRILL KUDRYAVTSEV)
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: Live Nation's settlement with DOJ still isn't a done deal
item.description: Live Nation has reached a settlement with the Department of Justice after a years-long antitrust battle. What could this mean for the broader live entertainment industry?
item.content:encoded: Live Nation has reached a settlement with the Department of Justice after a years-long antitrust battle. What could this mean for the broader live entertainment industry?
item.dc:creator: Kathryn Fink

item.title: World shares tumble as Iran war pushes crude prices over $110 a barrel
item.description: World shares tumbled on Monday, with Japan's benchmark Nikkei 225 index plunging more than 5%, after oil prices spiked at nearly $120 a barrel.
item.content:encoded:
World shares tumbled on Monday, with Japan's benchmark Nikkei 225 index plunging more than 5%, after oil prices spiked at nearly $120 a barrel.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Crude oil prices swing wildly as the Iran war stretches on
item.description: Brent crude reached its highest price since Russia invaded Ukraine in 2022. Gasoline prices in the U.S. are expected to continue to rise.
item.content:encoded:
Brent crude reached its highest price since Russia invaded Ukraine in 2022. Gasoline prices in the U.S. are expected to continue to rise.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske
